reason: Events historically split attendee-facing venue text, coordinates, and directions across meetingPoint, startingPointLat, startingPointLng, and locationDetails. Google Places and mandatory map selection need one coherent location contract while old clients and projections still depend on the legacy fields.

item.name: attendee-facing place or pinned-location name
item.notes: nullable host directions such as gate, entrance, or floor

phases.description: Add meetingLocation to the event document and create/update callable schemas while leaving legacy fields required for compatibility.
Create/edit event clients submit meetingLocation and mirror the legacy location fields from that object.
Event callables normalize meetingLocation, derive legacy fields, and synthesize meetingLocation from legacy payloads during the transition.

Use tool/data/backfill_event_meeting_locations.mjs to derive meetingLocation from complete legacy pairs, synchronize mirrors, and block rather than fabricate unresolved coordinates. Dev is clean at 146/146. Staging is empty. Production has 138 deterministic repairs and 9 historical blockers, so the global phase remains pending.

The Firestore schema, generated TypeScript interface, Dart Event/ExternalEvent domains, create/update writes, discovery projection, and dev Functions now require exact non-null coordinates. Create keeps legacy scalar compatibility for released clients. Production rollout remains pending until the 9 unresolved historical documents are handled.
Remove meetingPoint, startingPointLat, startingPointLng, and locationDetails from primary app code, contracts, and seed tooling only after old clients are no longer supported.

environmentEvidence.dev: 2026-07-13: 146 events scanned; 146 valid structured locations; 0 repairs; 0 blockers after seed and projection repair.
environmentEvidence.prod: 2026-07-16 read-only refresh: 272 events scanned; 125 valid structured locations; 138 deterministic repairs; 9 unresolved historical coordinate blockers; 0 warnings. No writes applied.

rulesAndIndexes.firestoreRules: No direct event writes are allowed, so callable validation owns this migration.
rulesAndIndexes.firestoreIndexes: No new indexes are required until the app queries by nested meetingLocation fields.
